title: pilosella brachiata subsp. leptocomoides
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. leptocomoides, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or Alpine Hawk's-beard, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as ulcers and inflammation. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving digestive health. Pilosella brachiata subsp. leptocomoides can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
